iterate_target: Leaderboard walk-forward champion on ETH/1h: kama_stoch_pullback_v1_8to1 (QScore 4.27, PF 1.857, DD 10.1, 42 trades)
next_cycle_focus: Do not spend another exploit cycle on the v2/v3/v4 ETH / 1h KAMA-Stoch variants in their current form. All three current-cycle refinements failed the walk-forward trade-count requirement with zero trades, so any future exploit work on this family should restart from the original champion baseline rather than refining these specific variants further.
rationale: Cycle 127 is closed as a failed exploit iteration set, but the family itself is not abandoned. All three current-cycle ETH / 1h KAMA-Stoch refinement variants (v2, v3, v4) produced zero trades in both insample and out-of-sample walk-forward testing and failed with low-trade-count flags, which means these particular refinements over-tightened or over-distorted a passing baseline. The correct lesson is to reject these variants, not to abandon the underlying champion family that already proved itself.
